days.leg: Calais → Reims (2.5 hours, champagne country roads)
Reims → Dijon (2 hours, scenic vineyard routes)
Dijon → Lyon (2.5 hours, Burgundy wine region)
Lyon → Avignon (2.5 hours, Rhône Valley roads)
Avignon → Antibes (2.5 hours, Provence to Côte d'Azur)
Antibes → Menton (1.5 hours, scenic coastal drive)

1.name: L'Écritoire
1.notes: Charming French bistro in old Calais with regional specialties and excellent wine selection.

1.name: Hôtel Meurice de Calais
1.notes: Elegant 4-star hotel overlooking the English Channel with fine dining restaurant.

2.name: Veuve Clicquot
2.notes: Iconic champagne house offering tours of historic cellars and tastings in Reims.

2.name: Restaurant Au Petit Comptoir
2.notes: Cozy Michelin-recommended bistro serving traditional Champenois cuisine.

2.name: Hôtel De La Paix
2.notes: Luxury 4-star hotel in the heart of Reims with spa and gourmet restaurant.

3.name: Route des Grands Crus
3.notes: Scenic drive through Burgundy's most prestigious vineyards between Dijon and Beaune.

3.name: Stephane Derbord
3.notes: Michelin-starred restaurant in Dijon specializing in contemporary French cuisine with Burgundy wines.

3.name: Hôtel Wilson
3.notes: Elegant 4-star hotel on Place Liberation with fine dining and regional wine cellar.

4.name: Institut Paul Bocuse
4.notes: Culinary institute and restaurant showcasing Lyon's legendary gastronomic heritage.

4.name: Le Passage
4.notes: Award-winning restaurant in Old Town offering innovative French cuisine with local ingredients.

4.name: Hôtel Villa Florentine
4.notes: Luxury 5-star Renaissance palace hotel with panoramic city views and Michelin-starred dining.

5.name: Palais des Papes
5.notes: UNESCO World Heritage medieval papal palace with stunning architecture and historical tours.

5.name: Christian Etienne
5.notes: Michelin-starred restaurant in Avignon serving Provençal cuisine with Mediterranean influences.

5.name: Hôtel de l'Horloge
5.notes: 4-star boutique hotel in the medieval center with rooftop restaurant overlooking the city.

6.name: Old Town Antibes
6.notes: Wander narrow medieval streets, visit Picasso Museum, and stroll the charming harbor promenade.

6.name: Bacon Restaurant
6.notes: Waterfront Michelin-starred restaurant specializing in Mediterranean seafood and local fish.

6.name: Hôtel du Cap-Eden-Roc
6.notes: Legendary 5-star luxury resort on the Cap d'Antibes with private beach and world-class dining.

7.name: Corniche de l'Estérel
7.notes: Thrilling coastal road with dramatic red rock formations and Mediterranean Sea views perfect for sports cars.

7.name: Le Méridien
7.notes: Elegant beachfront restaurant in Menton with fresh seafood and views of the Italian coast.

7.name: Hôtel Britannia
7.notes: Historic Belle Époque 4-star hotel overlooking Menton's pebble beach with fine dining.

title: 7-Day Porsche 911 Adventure: London to the Mediterranean
subtitle: London → Calais → Reims → Burgundy → Lyon → Provence → French Riviera
itineraryNotes:
A fun drive through France, starting in London.
